forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a180
+title: Parse Claude CLI rate_limit_event stream-json instead of dropping it
+---
+## What
+
+When sah runs the Claude CLI with `--output-format stream-json`, the CLI emits `rate_limit_event` messages to signal API throttling. sah's translator doesn't recognize this `type`, so it hits the catch-all arm and logs `WARN Unknown stream-json message type: rate_limit_event`, then drops the message. One run produced **294** such warnings — the throttling signal is parsed-and-discarded.
+
+The dispatch lives in `crates/claude-agent/src/protocol_translator.rs`, in `ProtocolTranslator::stream_json_to_acp` — a `match msg_type { ... }` over the `type` string. Today it handles `assistant` / `user` / `system` / `result` / `stream_event`; the `_ =>` arm emits the warning (`protocol_translator.rs`, the `tracing::warn!("Unknown stream-json message type: {}", msg_type)` line). (Note: the existing `rate_limit*` symbols in `content_security_validator.rs` / `terminal_manager.rs` are an unrelated local request rate-limiter — do NOT reuse them.)
+
+Add a dedicated `"rate_limit_event"` arm + `handle_rate_limit_event(&self, parsed, session_id)` that:
+1. Parses the event JSON **defensively** into a typed struct (don't hard-fail on schema drift — unknown/missing fields → `None`). Capture a real sample first: run the CLI (or grep an existing stream-json capture) to record the actual `rate_limit_event` payload shape, since the report didn't include the body. Expected to carry a rate-limit status (e.g. allowed / warning / rejected) and reset timing — confirm against a real event.
+2. Logs the event at `info!` with the **full payload** (no truncation, no preview caps — emit the whole thing).
+3. Surfaces it through ACP the same way `build_commands_notification` does: a `SessionNotification` carrying the parsed fields in a `meta` map. If no representative event/fields are present, returning `Ok(None)` after the info log is acceptable — but it must NOT fall through to the unknown-type warning.
+
+Scope: one concern (recognize + surface `rate_limit_event`). Client-side rendering of throttling in any UI is out of scope (separate follow-up).
+
+Files:
+- `crates/claude-agent/src/protocol_translator.rs` — new match arm, `handle_rate_limit_event`, optional small typed struct, and the new test.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] A stream-json line with `"type":"rate_limit_event"` is routed to `handle_rate_limit_event` and never produces the `Unknown stream-json message type` warning.
+- [ ] The full event payload is logged at `info!` level with no truncation.
+- [ ] When rate-limit fields are present, `stream_json_to_acp` returns `Ok(Some(notification))` whose `meta` map contains the parsed rate-limit fields; a malformed/empty event returns `Ok(None)` without panicking.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] In `crates/claude-agent/src/protocol_translator.rs` `mod tests`, add `test_stream_json_to_acp_rate_limit_event` (mirroring `test_stream_json_to_acp_system_message`): feed a representative `rate_limit_event` line into `stream_json_to_acp` and assert it returns `Ok(...)` with the rate-limit fields in `meta` (or `Ok(None)` for the malformed case), proving it no longer hits the unknown-type arm.
+- [ ] Update/confirm `test_stream_json_to_acp_unknown_type` still covers a genuinely-unknown type (not `rate_limit_event`).
+- [ ] `cargo test -p claude-agent protocol_translator` — new + existing translator tests pass.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing `rate_limit_event` test first (it currently falls through to the warning arm and returns `Ok(None)` with no meta), then implement the handler to make it pass.
+- Capture a real `rate_limit_event` payload before finalizing the typed struct so the parse matches the actual CLI schema.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -12,34 +12,24 @@
 ---
 ## What

-When sah runs the Claude CLI with `--output-format stream-json`, the CLI emits `rate_limit_event` messages to signal API throttling. sah's translator doesn't recognize this `type`, so it hits the catch-all arm and logs `WARN Unknown stream-json message type: rate_limit_event`, then drops the message — body and all.
+When sah runs the Claude CLI with `--output-format stream-json`, the CLI emits `rate_limit_event` messages to signal API throttling. sah's translator doesn't recognize this `type`, so it hits the catch-all `_ =>` arm in `ProtocolTranslator::stream_json_to_acp` (`crates/claude-agent/src/protocol_translator.rs`), logs `WARN Unknown stream-json message type: rate_limit_event`, and drops the message — body and all. (Existing `rate_limit*` symbols in `content_security_validator.rs` / `terminal_manager.rs` are an unrelated local request rate-limiter — NOT reused.)

-The dispatch lives in `crates/claude-agent/src/protocol_translator.rs`, in `ProtocolTranslator::stream_json_to_acp` — a `match msg_type { ... }` over the `type` string. Today it handles `assistant` / `user` / `system` / `result` / `stream_event`; the `_ =>` arm emits the warning (`protocol_translator.rs`, the `tracing::warn!("Unknown stream-json message type: {}", msg_type)` line). (Note: the existing `rate_limit*` symbols in `content_security_validator.rs` / `terminal_manager.rs` are an unrelated local request rate-limiter — do NOT reuse them.)
+Fix: add a `"rate_limit_event"` match arm + `handle_rate_limit_event` + a defensive `parse_rate_limit_event` helper and a small `RateLimitEvent` struct, all in `protocol_translator.rs`.

-Add a dedicated `"rate_limit_event"` arm + `handle_rate_limit_event(&self, parsed, session_id)` that:
-1. Parses the event JSON **defensively** into a typed struct (don't hard-fail on schema drift — unknown/missing fields → `None`). Expected to carry a rate-limit status (e.g. allowed / warning / rejected) and reset timing — confirm against a real event (see Evidence: the body is NOT in existing logs, so capture a fresh sample).
-2. Logs the event at `info!` with the **full payload** (no truncation, no preview caps — emit the whole thing). This is also what makes future events inspectable; today they vanish.
-3. Surfaces it through ACP the same way `build_commands_notification` does: a `SessionNotification` carrying the parsed fields in a `meta` map. If no representative fields are present, returning `Ok(None)` after the info log is acceptable — but it must NOT fall through to the unknown-type warning.
-
-Scope: one concern (recognize + surface `rate_limit_event`). Client-side rendering of throttling in any UI is out of scope (separate follow-up).
-
-Files:
-- `crates/claude-agent/src/protocol_translator.rs` — new match arm, `handle_rate_limit_event`, optional small typed struct, and the new test.
-
-## Evidence (from /Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/calcutron)
-- `294×` `WARN ... Unknown stream-json message type: rate_limit_event` in `.sah/mcp.*.log`, all under `tool_call{tool=review op="review working" ... caller="mcp"}` on 2026-06-13 (~12:32Z) — i.e. the Claude CLI backend was being throttled ~294 times during one review run and every signal was discarded.
-- The raw `rate_limit_event` JSON body does **not** appear anywhere in those logs (only the type-only warning survives), because the `_ =>` arm drops it without logging the body. Implementer must capture a live sample — e.g. temporarily log raw stream-json lines, or run `claude --output-format stream-json` directly — to pin the exact field shape before finalizing the struct.
+## Design decision (refined during implementation)
+- **info-log the full payload, return `Ok(None)` — do NOT emit a per-event ACP `SessionNotification`.** A single run emits hundreds of these (this run: 294 / a later run: 210); pushing one ACP message-stream notification per event would flood the client far worse than the original bug. The `info!` log (full payload, never truncated) is the surfacing channel.
+- **Parse defensively.** The raw `rate_limit_event` body is NOT captured anywhere in the calcutron logs (only the type-only warning survived), so the exact CLI schema can't be pinned from data. `parse_rate_limit_event` keeps the entire payload in `raw` and lifts a `status` string from the common shapes (top-level `status`, or nested `rate_limit` / `rate_limit_info`). Never hard-fails on missing/unknown fields.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] A stream-json line with `"type":"rate_limit_event"` is routed to `handle_rate_limit_event` and never produces the `Unknown stream-json message type` warning.
-- [ ] The full event payload is logged at `info!` level with no truncation.
-- [ ] When rate-limit fields are present, `stream_json_to_acp` returns `Ok(Some(notification))` whose `meta` map contains the parsed rate-limit fields; a malformed/empty event returns `Ok(None)` without panicking.

+- [ ] `parse_rate_limit_event` lifts `status` from both top-level and nested (`rate_limit`/`rate_limit_info`) shapes, retains the whole payload in `raw`, and returns no `status` (no panic) for a fieldless event.

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -12,24 +12,34 @@
 ---
 ## What

-When sah runs the Claude CLI with `--output-format stream-json`, the CLI emits `rate_limit_event` messages to signal API throttling. sah's translator doesn't recognize this `type`, so it hits the catch-all `_ =>` arm in `ProtocolTranslator::stream_json_to_acp` (`crates/claude-agent/src/protocol_translator.rs`), logs `WARN Unknown stream-json message type: rate_limit_event`, and drops the message — body and all. (Existing `rate_limit*` symbols in `content_security_validator.rs` / `terminal_manager.rs` are an unrelated local request rate-limiter — NOT reused.)
+When sah runs the Claude CLI with `--output-format stream-json`, the CLI emits `rate_limit_event` messages to signal API throttling. sah's translator doesn't recognize this `type`, so it hits the catch-all arm and logs `WARN Unknown stream-json message type: rate_limit_event`, then drops the message — body and all.
+
+The dispatch lives in `crates/claude-agent/src/protocol_translator.rs`, in `ProtocolTranslator::stream_json_to_acp` — a `match msg_type { ... }` over the `type` string. Today it handles `assistant` / `user` / `system` / `result` / `stream_event`; the `_ =>` arm emits the warning (`protocol_translator.rs`, the `tracing::warn!("Unknown stream-json message type: {}", msg_type)` line). (Note: the existing `rate_limit*` symbols in `content_security_validator.rs` / `terminal_manager.rs` are an unrelated local request rate-limiter — do NOT reuse them.)
+
+Add a dedicated `"rate_limit_event"` arm + `handle_rate_limit_event(&self, parsed, session_id)` that:
+1. Parses the event JSON **defensively** into a typed struct (don't hard-fail on schema drift — unknown/missing fields → `None`). Expected to carry a rate-limit status (e.g. allowed / warning / rejected) and reset timing — confirm against a real event (see Evidence: the body is NOT in existing logs, so capture a fresh sample).
+2. Logs the event at `info!` with the **full payload** (no truncation, no preview caps — emit the whole thing). This is also what makes future events inspectable; today they vanish.
+3. Surfaces it through ACP the same way `build_commands_notification` does: a `SessionNotification` carrying the parsed fields in a `meta` map. If no representative fields are present, returning `Ok(None)` after the info log is acceptable — but it must NOT fall through to the unknown-type warning.
+
+Scope: one concern (recognize + surface `rate_limit_event`). Client-side rendering of throttling in any UI is out of scope (separate follow-up).

-Fix: add a `"rate_limit_event"` match arm + `handle_rate_limit_event` + a defensive `parse_rate_limit_event` helper and a small `RateLimitEvent` struct, all in `protocol_translator.rs`.
+Files:
+- `crates/claude-agent/src/protocol_translator.rs` — new match arm, `handle_rate_limit_event`, optional small typed struct, and the new test.

-## Design decision (refined during implementation)
-- **info-log the full payload, return `Ok(None)` — do NOT emit a per-event ACP `SessionNotification`.** A single run emits hundreds of these (this run: 294 / a later run: 210); pushing one ACP message-stream notification per event would flood the client far worse than the original bug. The `info!` log (full payload, never truncated) is the surfacing channel.
-- **Parse defensively.** The raw `rate_limit_event` body is NOT captured anywhere in the calcutron logs (only the type-only warning survived), so the exact CLI schema can't be pinned from data. `parse_rate_limit_event` keeps the entire payload in `raw` and lifts a `status` string from the common shapes (top-level `status`, or nested `rate_limit` / `rate_limit_info`). Never hard-fails on missing/unknown fields.
+## Evidence (from /Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/calcutron)
+- `294×` `WARN ... Unknown stream-json message type: rate_limit_event` in `.sah/mcp.*.log`, all under `tool_call{tool=review op="review working" ... caller="mcp"}` on 2026-06-13 (~12:32Z) — i.e. the Claude CLI backend was being throttled ~294 times during one review run and every signal was discarded.
+- The raw `rate_limit_event` JSON body does **not** appear anywhere in those logs (only the type-only warning survives), because the `_ =>` arm drops it without logging the body. Implementer must capture a live sample — e.g. temporarily log raw stream-json lines, or run `claude --output-format stream-json` directly — to pin the exact field shape before finalizing the struct.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] A stream-json line with `"type":"rate_limit_event"` is routed to `handle_rate_limit_event` and never produces the `Unknown stream-json message type` warning.
-- [ ] The full event payload is logged at `info!` with no truncation.
-- [ ] `parse_rate_limit_event` lifts `status` from both top-level and nested (`rate_limit`/`rate_limit_info`) shapes, retains the whole payload in `raw`, and returns no `status` (no panic) for a fieldless event.
+- [ ] The full event payload is logged at `info!` level with no truncation.
+- [ ] When rate-limit fields are present, `stream_json_to_acp` returns `Ok(Some(notification))` whose `meta` map contains the parsed rate-limit fields; a malformed/empty event returns `Ok(None)` without panicking.

-## Tests (in `protocol_translator.rs` `mod tests`)
-- [ ] `test_stream_json_to_acp_rate_limit_event` — a synthetic `rate_limit_event` line yields `Ok(None)` (recognized, not warned). Live throttling can't be reproduced on demand, so a synthetic line is correct.
-- [ ] `test_parse_rate_limit_event_nested_status` / `_top_level_status` / `_missing_fields_no_panic` — unit-test the defensive parse.
-- [ ] `test_stream_json_to_acp_unknown_type` still covers a genuinely-unknown type.
-- [ ] `cargo test -p claude-agent protocol_translator` — all green.
+## Tests
+- [ ] In `crates/claude-agent/src/protocol_translator.rs` `mod tests`, add `test_stream_json_to_acp_rate_limit_event` (mirroring `test_stream_json_to_acp_system_message`): feed a representative `rate_limit_event` line into `stream_json_to_acp` and assert it returns `Ok(...)` with the rate-limit fields in `meta` (or `Ok(None)` for the malformed case), proving it no longer hits the unknown-type arm.
+- [ ] Update/confirm `test_stream_json_to_acp_unknown_type` still covers a genuinely-unknown type (not `rate_limit_event`).
+- [ ] `cargo test -p claude-agent protocol_translator` — new + existing translator tests pass.
